name: For the remains of the Civil War and the lime kilns — circular from Quijorna
name_original: Por los restos de la Guerra Civil y los hornos de cal — circular desde Quijorna
region_intro: Hiking around Quijorna offers access to a natural and rural environment approximately 39 kilometers from Madrid. The region is characterized by diverse countryside landscapes, including areas like Dehesa Boyal and Mesa de los Llanos. Mediterranean forests, primarily holm oak, along with various scrublands and the presence of rivers and arroyos, define the terrain. While not in the high mountains, Quijorna benefits from its proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama, providing varied hiking terrain. Best hiking trails around Quijorna The most popular hiking route is For the remains of the Civil War and the lime kilns — circular from Quijorna, an 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route features historical Civil War remnants and well-preserved lime kilns. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro Alarcón Reservoir and Puente del Pasadero Loop, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This trail offers scenic reservoir views and includes the historic Puente del Pasadero. Local hikers also love the Hunting Area Warning Sign – Bunker Remains at Peñas Pardas loop from Quijorna, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through natural landscapes with Civil War bunkers, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Quijorna is defined by Mediterranean forests, rivers and arroyos, and countryside landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Quijorna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Quijorna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Quijorna?
A: There are over 95 hiking routes in the Quijorna area, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix, with 35 easy trails, 56 moderate options, and 4 more challenging treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Quijorna?
A: Hiking around Quijorna offers a diverse natural environment. You'll encounter countryside landscapes, Mediterranean forests dominated by holm oaks, various scrublands like broom and rosemary, and scenic areas along rivers and arroyos. While not in the high mountains, its proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama adds to the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Quijorna?
A: Yes, Quijorna offers several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6 km path that takes about 1 hour 32 minutes, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Quijorna?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Quijorna are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1378651933/for-the-remains-of-the-civil-war-and-the-lime-kilns-circular-from-quijorna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;For the remains of the Civil War and the lime kilns — circular from Quijorna&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.6 km route that explores historical sites.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking in Quijorna?
A: Quijorna's trails often lead to remnants of the Spanish Civil War. You can discover well-preserved lime kilns and bunkers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21956258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hunting Area Warning Sign – Bunker Remains at Peñas Pardas loop from Quijorna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.3 km trail that takes you past Civil War bunkers.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the trails?
A: Yes, the area boasts scenic beauty, particularly around its rivers and arroyos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376186052/cerro-alarcon-reservoir-and-puente-del-pasadero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Alarcón Reservoir and Puente del Pasadero Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful reservoir views and features the historic Puente del Pasadero. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2666995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia River Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1393760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge over the Guadarrama River in Boadilla&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Quijorna?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Quijorna, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's natural and rural environment, the diverse landscapes, and the blend of historical interest with natural beauty.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting landmarks. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2863178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Lime Kiln of Quijorna&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/658577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen del Soto&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Quijorna?
A: Quijorna offers good hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the Mediterranean forests and countryside. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are typically mild.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the traditional Vías Pecuarias?
A: Yes, the municipality of Quijorna includes traditional livestock routes known as Vías Pecuarias, such as the Cañada Real Segoviana and the Vereda de los Montes. These ancient paths offer unique opportunities to explore the region's historical and natural landscapes.

Q: Is there wildlife to observe while hiking in Quijorna?
A: The natural environment around Quijorna supports diverse wildlife. You might spot wild boars, rabbits, common hedgehogs, and various bird species like white storks, kestrels, and hoopoes. Approximately half of Quijorna's municipal area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA).
tips:
Next to Alto de los Llanos are these ruins which, it is said, were the headquarters of the Republican command.
Very close we can also find the geodesic vertex of the place, which is located at 746 meters of altitude.

Balancing chimneys (also called oscillation wells or piezometric towers) are a type of auxiliary construction in hydraulic systems that act as pressure relief valves.
They are complementary structures to infrastructures that work with water and whose objective is to serve as a buffer against overpressures and underpressures in the canal.

The Picadas Canal is a water transport channel that connects the Picadas reservoir with the Majadahonda Potable Water Treatment Station (ETAP). It has a length of 49.2 km and its entry into service was in 1967.
It is the first major supply channel for Madrid that requires elevations, given that the catchment point (the Alberche River) is at a level much lower than that of the city of Madrid. It has two lift stations: Picadas and Colmenar del Arroyo I.
More at https://bigjoe.es/iii-marcha-del-cocido-de-quijorna-2016/

Surprising cave built by Republican soldiers with pick and shovel to be used as a barracks and bomb shelter. It is recommended to carry a flashlight with you if you want to admire the work that creating this cave must have involved.
The large number of galleries that come out to the right and left of the main one is surprising. A marvel that is in perfect condition.

This is one of the many bunkers that were on the Brunete front, in very good condition.
If you go around it, you will be able to see its access, which is located at the back.

This lime kiln will perhaps be the best preserved in the area. The cylindrical shape of the base draws a lot of attention, on which a conical shape rises with its gas outlet.
Because of this shape they are known as bottle ovens.
This refractory ceramic kiln uses kaolin to produce refractory bricks.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Republican Headquarters (Spanish Civil War)
Balancing Chimney of the Picadas Canal
